description:

02.19.2026 – CNN “Laura Coates Live.” Gloria Allred, famed victim’s rights attorney, joins Laura Coates in commenting on Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s arrest on suspicion of misconduct in public office. Ms. Allred claims that she has for years been publicly urging Mr. Mountbatten- Windsor to come forward and speak with law enforcement about his involvement with Jeffrey Epstein, which he “…never did.” Though there has been evidence of alleged child sex trafficking by Andrew Mountbatten- Windsor, no arrests have been made on those charges. Ms. Allred asks,“Where is the priority for crimes against women and children?” and added, “Perhaps it matters more when someone …is revealing a secret of the state.”
 
Please see the clip download link here: Download clip
 
 
02.20.2026 – The Guardian “Andrew’s arrest does not guarantee justice for trafficking victims, says top US lawyer.” Gloria Allred, a women’s rights lawyer representing many victims of Jeffrey Epstein, has said, “…there still has not been any action on the part of the police on allegations that Andrew has committed crimes against women.” Ms. Allred said that while the UK had acted quickly on the allegation that the former prince had shared confidential documents with the disgraced financier while he was a trade envoy, there appeared to be far less progress on sexual assault allegations against him. Ms. Allred stated, “Nevertheless, the UK is ahead of the United States in demanding some accountability from powerful men who were part of Jeffrey Epstein’s social and business circles.”
Andrew’s arrest does not guarantee justice for trafficking victims, says top US lawyer | UK news | The Guardian
 
 
 
02.20.2026 – Chicago Today “Playboy Founder's Widow Alleges Underage Photos in Foundation's Collection.” Crystal Hefner, widow of Playboy publisher Hugh Hefner, and Gloria Allred, a victim’s rights attorney representing Crystal Hefner, has filed regulatory complaints with the California and Illinois Attorney General's offices to prevent any potential distribution of the scrapbook images that are in the possession of the Hugh M. Hefner Foundation. According to Ms. Hefner and Ms. Allred, the 3,000 scrapbooks that may contain “…thousands of nude images of women and Hefner's diary, which contains highly personal information regarding his sexual exploits.”  Ms. Hefner expressed concerns as she alleged that these books may contain images of girls who may have been underaged at the time.
Playboy Founder's Widow Alleges Underage Photos in Foundation's Collection - Chicago Today
 
 
 
02.20.2026 – The Independent  “Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor latest: Met Police contacting former bodyguards as part of Epstein files probe” Gloria Allred, a women’s rights lawyer who represents many victims of Jeffrey Epstein, told BBC Radio 4’s Today programme: “The state has acted quickly to make this arrest but there still has not been any action on the part of the police on allegations that Andrew has committed crimes against women.” Ms. Allred has said that she does not believe there will be any “any real justice” for sex trafficking victims of Jeffrey Epstein and accountability for other high profile individuals involved, despite Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s arrest on Thursday. Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor latest: Met Police contacting former bodyguards as part of Epstein files probe | The Independent
 
 
 
02.20.2026 – NewsNation “Photo shows former Prince Andrew after arrest on suspicion of misconduct in public office.” Following the arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor on suspicion of misconduct in public office, Attorney Gloria Allred, who represents many victims of Jeffrey Epstein, released a statement: “The arrest today of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor (formerly known as Prince) is for a crime against the State and the State has acted quickly to make this arrest.  However, there has still not been any action on the part of the police on allegations that Andrew has committed crimes against women.” Ms. Allred continued, “Nevertheless, the U.K. is ahead of the United States in demanding some accountability from powerful men who were part of Jeffrey Epstein’s social and business circle.”
Prince Andrew arrest: Photo shows former prince leaving police station
 
 
 
02.20.2026 – The National “UK parliament under pressure to remove Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor from line of succession.” British M.P.s are facing mounting pressure to remove Andrew Mountbatten- Windsor from the line of succession to the throne following his arrest for alleged misconduct in public office. U.S. attorney Gloria Allred, who has represented many survivors of Epstein’s abuse, noted the speed with which authorities appeared to act on allegations concerning state or financial matters compared with claims of sexual abuse. “The state is acting quickly,” she said, “but there still has not been any action on the part of the police on allegations that Andrew has committed crimes against women.”
 
 UK parliament under pressure to remove Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor from line of succession | The National
 
 
 
02.20.2026 – USA Today “Attorney Gloria Allred says America needs to demand accountability from powerful men.” Attorney Gloria Allred, who represents several of Epstein's victims, says authorities need to act not just on allegations of financial crimes, but those of rape and sexual abuse. "Apparently, allegations concerning State trade secrets and/or allegations of financial crimes are given priority and quick action,” she said in a Feb. 19 statement to USA TODAY “while allegations of rape, child sexual abuse and sex trafficking take many years to investigate and do not result in arrests and accountability for victims who are women and girls.”
Prince Andrew arrested, released amid Epstein ties: Live updates
 
 
 
02.20.2026 – OK! “Hugh Hefner's Widow Crystal Warns Explicit Photos in Playboy Mogul's Scrapbooks May Include Underage Girls.” Crystal Hefner, his third wife and widow, has filed regulatory complaints with the Attorney Generals of California and Illinois, alleging concerns over the Hugh M. Hefner Foundation possession of approximately 3,000 personal scrapbooks that contain nude photographs of women, and that the materials pose serious privacy and consent concerns. Victim’s rights attorney Gloria Allred held a press conference on February 17th with her client, Ms. Hefner, to discuss these concerns.  
 
Hugh Hefner’s Scrapbooks May Include Images Of Underage Girls: Widow
 
 
 
 
02.20.2026 – Complex “Crystal Hefner Fires Back at Hugh Hefner's Sons: 'I Too Have Seen the Images'” Crystal Hefner disputed Cooper and Marston Hefner’s response to her recent press conference with high profile women’s rights attorney, Gloria Allred, regarding Hugh Hefner’s private scrapbooks and the concerns Ms. Hefner raised about the personal photos of women contained in them.  Crystal has said some of those images may involve individuals who could not provide full consent, and she has raised concerns about the possibility that minors are depicted.
Crystal Hefner Responds to Hugh Hefner’s Sons: ‘I Too Have Seen the Images’

Ms. Arden hopes that any high-profile figures that are asked to testify before Congress will do so. Ms. Allred also expressed anger at how this process was mishandled and chaotic, and wondered if it could be that victims were being punished for reporting these abuses?  Ms. Allred said it is outrageous and disgusting that nude photos of victims were released in these documents, and that victims continue to not be protected.

02.02.2026 - BBC“ Andrew under pressure to give evidence on Epstein.” Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor faces growing pressure to give evidence of what he knows about Jeffrey Epstein, with royal sources saying: "Anyone who has information should consider helping any investigation." The latest emails released by the U.S. Department of Justice reveal there have been multiple unsuccessful approaches from U.S. authorities for Andrew to help with Epstein inquiries. Gloria Allred, a prominent lawyer representing some of the victims of Jeffrey Epstein, added to the calls, saying: "It's not too late and he does have information he could share." “Why was he willing to speak to the BBC in a one-hour catastrophic television interview but is not willing to speak to law enforcement?," Ms. Allred asked, referring to Andrew's now-infamous Newsnight interview with Emily Maitlis in 2019.

Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor under pressure to give evidence on Jeffrey Epstein
02.02.2026 – PBS News “Epstein victims want former Prince Andrew to testify before lawmakers. He's unlikely to do so.” Former Prince Andrew saw his reputation destroyed six years ago when he gave a disastrous interview to the BBC about his relationship with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The pressure for Andrew to testify is growing after the latest release of documents from the U.S. Justice Department's investigation into Mr. Epstein revealed further unsavory details about links between the two men.  Attorney Gloria Allred, who represents many of Epstein's victims, said on Monday that Andrew has a duty to provide any evidence that could help investigators understand how Jeffrey Epstein was able to abuse so many women for so long, and who else might have been involved in his crimes. "He's not the one who should decide whether he knows anything that could help in the investigation," Ms. Allred said. Seeking help: Please make the following  updates to attorney Christina Cheung's bio. 

Domain: amglaw.com

Attorney profile link: https://www.amglaw.com/attorneys/christina-cheung/

Requirements for completion:

Review the provided copy updates and replace them on Christina's profile. There are 3 updates.

1. Add this text next to profile picture: 

 
Christina Cheung, Partner, Employment Law Attorney-Sexual Harassment, Discrimination & Child Sexual Abuse Cases

 

 

2. Add this text in the "MEET CHRISTINA"  section:

Attorney Christina Cheung is a California and New York employment law attorney specializing in sexual harassment, sexual assault, workplace pregnancy and disability discrimination, retaliation, and wrongful termination cases on behalf of employees. She also represents survivors of sexual abuse by teachers, coaches, police officers, and other authority figures in institutional settings. Her practice focuses exclusively on representing plaintiffs—never employers or institutions—in civil rights litigation.
 
Since joining Allred, Maroko & Goldberg in September 2011, Ms. Cheung has successfully represented hundreds of clients in sexual harassment, assault, and employment discrimination cases, recovering hundreds of millions for them. Notable results include a $5.5 million settlement for a survivor allegedly sexually assaulted by a police officer, a $2.25 million settlement against a school district on behalf of a teenager who alleged she was sexually assaulted by her teacher, and a $9 million judgment in a child sexual abuse case.
 
Ms. Cheung previously worked as a trial attorney in the U.S. Department of Justice’s Civil Rights Division, Employment Litigation Section, where she prosecuted systemic employment discrimination cases on behalf of the federal government. Ms. Cheung graduated near the top of her class from UCLA School of Law, where she was mentored by Vicki Schultz, one of the nation’s leading scholars in employment discrimination and sexual harassment law. She earned her Bachelor of Science in Foreign Service from Georgetown University, and studied abroad at the Universidad Autónoma de Madrid, where she became fluent in Spanish.
 
As a prominent leader in the California employment law community, Ms. Cheung serves on the Board of Directors of the California Employment Lawyers Association, one of California’s most influential employment law organizations. She also serves on the Executive Boards of two nonprofits: the Foundation for Advocacy, Inclusion & Resources and Legal Aid at Work, a leading organization supporting vulnerable workers. Additionally, Ms. Cheung serves on the Executive Committee of the Los Angeles County Bar Association’s Labor & Employment Section.
 
Ms. Cheung is frequently invited to speak at major legal conferences, where she shares her expertise on sexual harassment, discrimination, and retaliation. She has received numerous honors, including recognition as a Super Lawyer Rising Star (2017 to 2021) and Southern California Super Lawyer (2023-present)—an honor awarded to no more than 5% of the lawyers in the state each year. She has also been named to Best Lawyers in Employment Law (top 5% of private practice attorneys nationwide), and LawDragon’s Top 500 Leading Civil Rights & Plaintiff Employment Lawyers (nationwide).
 
As a certified 200-hour yoga instructor, Ms. Cheung brings a trauma-informed approach to her legal practice, drawing on this training to support survivors of sexual harassment, assault, and abuse. She understands that representing trauma survivors requires both aggressive advocacy and respect for each client’s healing journey.
 
 

3. Add this information to the "CREDENTIALS” section:

 
Speaking Engagements & Thought Leadership
 
Ms. Cheung is a recognized speaker and thought leader in employment law, regularly presenting at prestigious legal conferences. Ms. Cheung’s recent speaking engagements since 2020 include:
 
“Building the Record Without Breaking the Client: Trauma-Informed Litigation Practice,” American Conference Institute, February 2026

01.19.2026 - The Guardian – “Epstein victims’ advocates express outrage over slow release of files.” One month after the Justice Department’s congressionally mandated deadline to release all Jeffrey Epstein investigative files went unmet, victims’ advocates expressed outrage over the Department’s delays and failure to comply with federal law.  Justice Department attorneys said that they have publicly uploaded 12,285 documents to the DOJ website but stated that Department staff had found “…more than 2 million documents potentially responsive to the act that are in various phases of review.”  “As lawyers we often say, ‘Justice delayed is justice denied,’” Gloria Allred, an attorney who has represented numerous Epstein survivors, said. title: Changes to AMG website to Reflect Nathan's Passing
description:
Please see the following changes from the client.

  1. Remove Nathan’s photo from the roulette of attorneys on the home page: Best Employment Lawyer & Employment Rights Law Firm | LA & NYC


 2. Modify attorney page to state (in memoriam—1946-2025) under Nathan’s profile picture: Attorneys Archive - Allred, Maroko & Goldberg
Add (in memoriam—1946-2025) below Nathan’s name 






3. On Nathan’s individual attorney profile page: Nathan Goldberg - Allred, Maroko & Goldberg replace the “meet Nathan” tab with “in memoriam of Nathan” and the below tribute:
 
In Memoriam: Nathan Goldberg
 
Founding Partner
 
We honor the life, legacy, and extraordinary career of Nathan Goldberg, one of the founding partners of Allred, Maroko & Goldberg, whose dedication to justice and unwavering advocacy helped define our firm and the field of employment law in California.
 
Nathan was widely recognized as one of California’s preeminent plaintiff-side employment lawyers. The Daily Journal named him one of the Top 10 Plaintiff Employment Lawyers in California, a distinction that reflected both his legal excellence and his profound commitment to representing working people.
 
A graduate of Loyola Law School, where he earned his degree summa cum laude in 1974, Nathan combined intellectual rigor with courtroom mastery. He spent his life fighting for (mostly) women’s rights. For decades, he was named one of the Top 100 Southern California “Super Lawyers” by Law & Politics and Los Angeles Magazine, every year since 2004. He was also recognized by LawDragon as one of the 500 Leading Plaintiff Lawyers in America.
 
Throughout his career, Nathan secured landmark verdicts that reshaped lives and held powerful institutions accountable. In 2019, he obtained two verdicts against Alki David, one for $58.25 million. In 2006, he obtained an $18 million jury verdict on behalf of a Vons employee who was wrongfully terminated after reporting sexual harassment. He served as lead trial counsel in numerous high-impact cases, including Adams v. Los Angeles Unified School District and Salama v. King Drew Medical Center, each resulting in jury awards exceeding $4 million. Nathan also achieved significant victories in cases such as Tylo v. Spelling, a pregnancy discrimination trial that resulted in a nearly $5 million verdict, and Thorne v. City of El Segundo, in which he served as both trial and appellate counsel. In Thomas v. City of Los Angeles, he successfully represented the wrongfully terminated director of the Los Angeles Zoo, securing a mandatory injunction restoring his client to his position and full relief for the violation of due process.
 
Beyond the courtroom, Nathan was a respected educator and leader in the legal community. He frequently spoke on employment law issues and served on numerous Continuing Education of the Bar (CEB) and Los Angeles County panels, generously sharing his knowledge with fellow attorneys.
 
Nathan’s legacy lives on through the countless clients he championed, the precedents he helped establish, and the values he instilled at our firm—integrity, courage, and an unrelenting pursuit of justice. He will be deeply missed and forever remembered.
 

4. Add an entry to the Firm Headlines page In The News | Allred, Maroko & Goldberg | Los Angeles & New York  that AMG is proud to announce that Olivia Flechsig has been promoted to Partner and that Byron Lau has been promoted to Counsel at Allred Maroko & Goldberg. We congratulate Olivia and Byron on their well-deserved achievements celebrating their contributions to our firm.

description:

12.20.2025 AOL – TIME -  “Incomplete Epstein Files Release Draws Criticism.”  Speaking to CNN, Gloria Allred, a lawyer who has represented more than 20 survivors of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse, noted that “there was resistance by this administration to releasing the Epstein files.” She asked, “The question is, is there a coverup?" and "What are they hiding?”  While the full impact of the thousands of photos and documents connected with Epstein that the DOJ released on Friday remains unclear at this time, given the sheer volume of materials, reviews so far suggest they include no major new revelations.
Incomplete Epstein Files Release Draws Criticism
 
 
12.20.2025 CNN – “Trump DOJ Releases Trove of Heavily Redacted Epstein Files.” Victims’ Rights Attorney Gloria Allred speaks with Fredericka Whitfield on the release and the redactions made on the files. Ms. Allred argues that it is imperative that the files are properly redacted to ensure the protection of the victims’ identities, and for justice to be finally brought for the survivors.
 https://f.io/bWaIkBAA
  
 
12.20.2025 – The Hill – “16 Epstein files, including photo of Trump, disappear from DOJ website: Report.” The Department of Justice told the public on its website that if it released information that “includes non-public personally identifiable information or other sensitive content,” then the public should contact the DOJ if they find any “information that should not have been posted.” It is not clear if that is why some files were removed, but Victim’s rights attorney, Gloria Allred, indicated that her firm’s legal team contacted the DOJ on Saturday about names seen in the files, “which should have been redacted.”
16 Epstein files, including photo of Donald Trump, disappear from DOJ website: Report
 
 
12.20.2025 – CNN Politics – “Attorney for Epstein’s survivors says the ‘system has failed.’”  Gloria Allred, an attorney who represents several of the women abused by Jeffrey Epstein, said Saturday she believes the “system has failed the survivors” as she demands more transparency regarding the Justice Department’s redactions. Ms. Allred said her legal team notified the department about the inclusion of names the DOJ was instructed not to include in the files, under the Epstein Files Transparency Act.  The Victim’s rights attorney is also concerned with the images of some of the potential victims and survivors which should have been but were not redacted in the released materials.
 
December 20, 2025 — Trump administration updates | CNN Politics
 
 
 
12.21.2025 CTV – “Concerns grow about ‘over redaction’ of Epstein files: Allred.” Lawyer Gloria Allred says she initially had concerns about victims’ identifying information not being censored, but now additional redactions are “feeding into conspiracy theories.”
Concerns grow about ‘over redaction’ of Epstein files: Allred
 
 
12.21.2025 Baller Alert – “DOJ Pulls Epstein Files After Trump Photos Surface.” The Justice Department is facing scrutiny after dozens of Jeffrey Epstein-related files vanished from its public website just one day after being released. According to The Associated Press, at least 16 files that were publicly accessible on Friday were no longer available as of Saturday. Attorney Gloria Allred shared that her team had contacted the DOJ after being alerted by individuals who noticed personal sensitive information in the files. This implies that the missing files may have included unredacted names of people connected to the Epstein investigation.
DOJ Removes Epstein Files After Trump Photos Spark Questions

Review to use:
Andrea Quiroga

I had the incredible privilege of being represented by Gloria Allred and Olivia Flechsig during one of the most challenging and transformative chapters of my life. Together, they helped me take legal action against the Miss Universe Organization, which had previously enforced a discriminatory rule barring mothers from competing in their pageants. As someone who became a mother at 19, learning that I was ineligible for something I had dreamed of since childhood was heartbreaking.

Olivia was compassionate, incredibly responsive, and always took the time to make sure I fully understood each step. She listened to me with patience and care, and her legal expertise consistently put me at ease. I felt seen, heard, and most importantly, supported. Having Gloria Allred on my team was beyond a dream. From the moment we met, she believed in me and helped me believe in myself. She is fierce, passionate, and deeply committed to justice. Her decades of experience and presence in the courtroom gave me the strength to use my voice without fear.

Working with this firm didn’t just help me fight for my rights it allowed me to stand up for women everywhere who have been unfairly excluded or silenced. I’m forever grateful for the care, professionalism, and heart that both Olivia and Gloria brought to my case. I cannot recommend Allred, Maroko & Goldberg enough. If you are seeking a team that will advocate for you with skill, integrity, and empathy, this is the team you want by your side.
Thanks much!

Title: Lawdragon Names Five AMG Attorneys as Among the 500 Leading Civil Rights & Plaintiff Employment Lawyers in the Country for 2025

Content: 
These lawyers are champions who stand tall and deliver justice to individuals who have lost their means to make a living, their freedom, their dignity. To have a job, a chance. To have one’s freedom and be able to speak one’s mind. To be able to proceed in life free of sexual abuse, harassment, discrimination and other boulders unfairly forced onto the paths of individuals trying to live their lives – that is the calling of these heroes.

Because while ‘life happens’ these are the lawyers who call out and hold to account those whose callous, greedy or vicious actions deprive others of their right to work, to exist, to peaceably abide. To go to court and win the respect that’s been stolen.

They’re a little busy just now.

$4 million verdict-- Allred Maroko & Goldberg attorneys Dolores Y. Leal and Olivia Flechsig obtained a $4M unanimous federal court jury verdict on behalf of Mark Snookal against Chevron U.S.A. Inc. in an employment disability discrimination case.

Chevron U.S.A. Inc. rescinded a job offer to work in Escravos Nigeria because of his heart condition -- “dilated aortic root.” Chevron defended the case arguing that Mr. Snookal  “posed an immediate and substantial degree of risk to his health or safety or the health or safety of others.”

Dolores Y. Leal and Olivia Flechsig were able to establish that it was not a defense to assert that Mr. Snookal has a disability with a future risk, since his disability did not presently interfere with his ability to perform the job in a manner that will not endanger him or others. 

Mark Snookal v. Chevron U.S.A. Inc. , Case No. 2:23-CV-6302-HDV-AJR (U.S. District Court Central District of California)."

06.11.25 – IMDB – “Harvey Weinstein Rape Retrial Descends Into Chaos Amid Split Verdicts, Jury Tensions & Threats”   A week into jury deliberations on Harvey Weinstein’s New York rape retrial, the proceedings appear to be descending into chaos.
Under personal and judicial duress, the jurors just informed Judge Curtis Farber that they have reached a split verdict: guilty on Count 1 of a criminal sexual act in the first degree against Miriam Haley, not guilty of the same charge involving Kaja Sokola and no verdict on Jessica Mann. In this retrial, each count of first-degree criminal sexual act carries a maximum sentence of 25 years. Ms. Haley, appearing outside the courthouse with her lawyer, Gloria Allred, said she was “grateful ” to the jury.

Alicia Arden, who said Jeffrey Epstein sexually battered her in the late 1990s and filed a police report at that time, held a news conference in Los Angeles with her attorney, Gloria Allred.  Ms. Arden said she would support the release of additional materials related to the case.  But Ms. Arden also expressed outrage at the possibility that Ghislaine Maxwell could receive clemency or other special treatment through the process, adding that the Justice Department’s approach had been “very upsetting” so far.

title: Content focus/update
assignees: Clayton Coombs, Ethan Vandenberg, Ovidiu Dita
description:
Christina with AMG sent over some new laws passed in California that she'd like us to create content focused on.
let me know if we need to get more info from the client.

Directly from the client:

"Governor Newsom passed a slew of bills that affect our practice areas—this article recaps them nicely: California 2025 Bill Signing Wrap Up: New Employment Laws | California Peculiarities Employment Law Blog
 
Could your team focus on creating content around these new laws? Particularly, AB250 ion which there is now a lookback window (between Jan 1, 2026 to Dec. 31, 2027) for survivors of adult sexual abuse to bring claims no matter when the abuse occurred.

Because AMG doesn’t focus on W&H law, the other recently passed bills to highlight in addition to AB250 are:
 
 
  1. Paid Family Leave Benefits to Care for “Designated Persons” (SB 590)
Starting July 1, 2028, California will allow eligible employees to receive paid family leave benefits to care for a seriously ill “designated person” – meaning any blood relative or individual who is the equivalent of family, even if the individual is not biologically or legally related to the employee.
For our full FP deep dive on SB 590, click here.
 
  1. Updates to Job Posting Requirements and Equal Pay Act (SB 642)
This new law updates California’s requirement to provide pay scales in job postings by redefining “pay scale” to mean “a good faith estimate of the salary or hourly wage range that the employer reasonably expects to pay for the position upon hire.” The law also makes significant changes to California’s Equal Pay Act, including extending the statute of limitations for pay discrimination claims to three years and allowing potential recovery of lost wages up to six years.
For our full FP deep dive on SB 642, click here.
 
  1. Cal-WARN Notice Changes (SB 617)
Layoffs, facility closures, and employee relocations can potentially trigger an employer’s duty to give employees advance notice of a layoff or certain other workplace events, and a new law expands what’s required to be included in those notices.
Learn more here.
 
  1. SB 513 – Personnel Records
SB 513 requires that personnel records relating to the employee’s performance include education and training records and require the employer ensure those records contain the following information: employee name, training provider name, the duration and date of the training, core competencies of a training – including skills in equipment or software – and the resulting certification or qualification.
This bill amends Section 1198.5 of the Labor Code.
 
  1. AB 406 by Assemblymember Pilar Schiavo (D-Baldwin Park) – Employment: unlawful discrimination: victims of violence--AB 406 expands the reasons for use of California Paid Sick Leave under the Healthy Workplaces Healthy Families Act of 2014 (“HWHFA”) effective October 1, 2025, and amends the state’s unpaid job-protected leave to include these additional reasons effective January 1, 2026. These amendments continue the state’s recent trend of annual updates to paid sick leave, which was significantly revised in 2024 and 2025.
 
The most recent changes allow employees to use paid sick leave and protected unpaid leave if they or a family member are a victim of certain crimes and are attending judicial proceedings related to that crime. Such judicial proceedings include, but are not limited to, any delinquency proceeding, a post-arrest release decision, plea, sentencing, postconviction release decision, or any proceeding where a right of that person is an issue. The term victim in this specific covered reason is defined as a person against whom a violent felony, serious felony, and/or felony theft or embezzlement is committed, as well as a person who suffers direct or threatened physical, psychological, or financial harm due to the commission or attempted commission or specific crimes or delinquent acts.
 
The bill also amends the existing jury duty leave law, to remove the requirement that employees must provide reasonable notice prior to taking time off to serve on a jury. However, if an employee uses paid sick leave or unpaid job-protected leave for jury duty, the same notice standard applies for this covered reason as for other covered reasons of use under these provisions, i.e. reasonable advance notice unless advance notice is not feasible.
 
  1. SB 303 – Bias Mitigation Training
SB 303 provides that an employee’s assessment, testing, admission, or acknowledgment of their own personal bias that was made in good faith and solicited or required as part of a bias mitigation training does not constitute unlawful discrimination under FEHA.
This bill adds Section 12940.2 to the Government Code.
 
  1. SB 477 by Senator Catherine Blakespear (D-Encinitas) – California Fair Employment and Housing Act: enforcement procedures
 
  1. AB 692 makes it unlawful for contracts entered into on or after January 1, 2026, to require the worker pay an employer a debt if the worker’s employment or work relationship terminates. The bill authorizes a private right of action and specifies civil penalties. This law does not apply to certain agreements, including those involving discretionary bonuses or relocation payments, provided they meet set criteria, including: (1) repayment terms must be in a separate agreement from the primary employment contract; (2) the worker must be advised of the right to consult an attorney and given at least 5 business days to do so before signing; (3) any repayment obligation for early separation must be prorated based on the remaining retention period (up to 2 years) and cannot accrue interest; (4) the worker must have the option to defer receipt of the payment until the end of the retention period without repayment obligation; and (5) repayment may only apply if the employee leaves voluntarily or is terminated for misconduct. Any person in violation of the prohibitions set forth in this bill shall be liable for the greater of a worker’s actual damages or up to $5,000 in penalties per worker, injunctive relief, and attorneys’ fees and costs.
 
This bill adds Section 16608 to the Business and Professions Code, and Section 926 to the Labor Code."
